evals.prompt: /design
evals.expected_output: A concise gallery of design modes and example invocations.
assertions: Agent dispatches to the empty-args handler.
Agent lists Audit, Polish, Component, Surface, System, Refactor, AI Interface, Badge Surface, 3D Interface, and Parallel Design Team modes.
Agent does not guess a mode when no arguments are provided.

evals.prompt: /design make the checkout page feel clearer on mobile and verify it in browser
evals.expected_output: A Surface + Polish workflow inferred from the natural-language request, with Chrome DevTools MCP proof expected when available.
assertions: Agent infers a primary mode without requiring an explicit mode token.
Agent names Surface as the primary mode and Polish or Rendered Proof as secondary modules.
Agent considers mobile layout, clarity, content resilience, and accessibility.
Agent uses Chrome DevTools MCP as the default browser proof path when available.

evals.prompt: /design audit /app/research-assistant for citation trust and keyboard bugs
evals.expected_output: An Audit + AI Interface workflow where the explicit audit hint remains primary while trust and keyboard modules are attached.
assertions: Agent treats audit as the primary mode because it is explicit.
Agent attaches AI Interface trust/provenance checks from the target context.
Agent attaches accessibility and keyboard/focus checks from the request.
Agent keeps the audit read-only unless implementation is requested.

evals.prompt: /design refactor src/app/dashboard/page.tsx and prove the table does not overflow
evals.expected_output: A Refactor + Surface + Chrome Proof workflow that edits existing UI only after inspection and verifies overflow in the browser.
assertions: Agent selects Refactor as the primary mode from the existing frontend path.
Agent attaches Surface/dashboard and rendered-proof modules.
Agent inspects existing tokens, components, table behavior, and responsive constraints before editing.
Agent expects Chrome DevTools MCP snapshot and desktop/mobile screenshot or equivalent proof for overflow.

evals.prompt: /design audit http://localhost:3000/settings for focus order and visual clipping
evals.expected_output: A Chrome DevTools MCP-first audit that selects or navigates the page, snapshots the accessibility tree, checks focus, console/network, and screenshots.
assertions: Agent uses Chrome DevTools MCP before generic screenshots when the tools are available.
Agent includes page selection or navigation, wait, take_snapshot, console, and network checks.
Agent verifies focus order with keyboard interaction and fresh snapshots.
Agent captures desktop and mobile visual evidence or states the exact blocker.

evals.prompt: /design polish the landing page, but Chrome DevTools MCP is not available in this harness
evals.expected_output: A proof workflow that records the MCP blocker and falls back to the next available rendered proof without mutating MCP setup.
assertions: Agent does not edit MCP registry, harness config, or install browser tooling from this skill.
Agent states Chrome DevTools MCP is unavailable as a proof blocker.
Agent falls back to local browser, Storybook, Playwright, screenshots, preview output, or static scanner evidence.
Agent reports the attempted proof path and fallback proof.

evals.prompt: /design check the modal accessibility on /billing with Chrome proof
evals.expected_output: An accessibility-facing design audit using snapshots, keyboard traversal, focus return, and visual focus evidence inside /design.
assertions: Agent keeps the work inside /design instead of invoking a deleted chrome-devtools-a11y skill.
Agent treats the accessibility tree as primary evidence for roles, names, headings, and state.
Agent verifies keyboard traversal with repeated key movement and fresh snapshots.
Agent checks focus trap or focus return for the modal.

evals.prompt: /design audit the hero image because the landing page feels slow
evals.expected_output: A UI-facing LCP/hero proof workflow that identifies the actual visible hero or LCP candidate before recommending visual or loading fixes.
assertions: Agent keeps UI-facing hero/LCP proof inside /design instead of handing off to a deleted LCP Chrome skill.
Agent identifies the actual LCP or hero candidate before recommending fixes.
Agent considers resource discovery, lazy loading, priority, render blocking, and main-thread delay.
Agent routes deep non-UI performance profiling outside /design.

evals.prompt: /design review this image preview drawer for memory leaks after repeated open and close
evals.expected_output: A UI lifecycle smell workflow that repeats the interaction, watches for retained UI resources, and avoids reading raw heap snapshots.
assertions: Agent keeps UI-facing lifecycle smell checks inside /design.
Agent repeats the relevant interaction to amplify possible leaks.
Agent checks detached DOM nodes, unremoved listeners, media/canvas cleanup, and animation loops.
Agent refuses to read raw heap snapshots into context and routes deep profiling to performance-profiler.

evals.prompt: /design debug why Chrome DevTools MCP cannot connect to my Claude config
evals.expected_output: A redirect because MCP setup and harness configuration troubleshooting is not frontend interface design work.
assertions: Agent detects MCP setup/config troubleshooting rather than UI design proof.
Agent does not mutate MCP config from /design.
Agent redirects to the appropriate harness or configuration workflow.

evals.prompt: /design use Chrome to scrape all links from this page
evals.expected_output: A refusal or redirect because generic browser automation and scraping are outside /design scope.
assertions: Agent detects generic browser automation rather than user-facing interface design.
Agent does not route to Chrome proof merely because Chrome is mentioned.
Agent redirects or refuses the scraping task.

evals.prompt: /design build an admin dashboard for support ticket triage
evals.expected_output: A product interface workflow with restrained, dense, scannable dashboard defaults and rendered proof.
assertions: Agent states a design thesis before implementation.
Agent classifies the surface as an operational product/dashboard.
Agent chooses restrained, dense, predictable defaults instead of marketing-style composition.
Agent includes loading, empty, error, long-content, and keyboard/focus states.
Agent requires rendered proof at desktop and mobile viewports when available.

evals.prompt: /design build a launch hero for a new spatial audio instrument
evals.expected_output: A brand or marketing workflow with a distinct visual thesis, real media/assets, and accessibility constraints.
assertions: Agent treats the request as brand/marketing or editorial rather than dashboard work.
Agent defines a visual thesis and signature design move.
Agent uses assets or product-specific media rather than generic abstract decoration.
Agent keeps text readable and verifies contrast.
Agent avoids generic purple-blue gradient/card-heavy AI defaults.

evals.prompt: /design component settings dropdown in a shadcn project
evals.expected_output: A component workflow that checks components.json and existing shadcn/Radix primitives before custom UI.
assertions: Agent detects or asks for project component-library context.
Agent checks components.json and existing components before inventing a new primitive.
Agent prefers shadcn/Radix for dropdown behavior.
Agent includes keyboard, focus, labels, and responsive behavior.

evals.prompt: /design an AI research assistant result page with citations and follow-up controls
evals.expected_output: An AI interface design workflow with provenance, tuners, governors, status, and recovery affordances.
assertions: Agent loads or applies AI UX patterns.
Agent includes source provenance and uncertainty handling.
Agent includes user controls for scope, follow-ups, and correction.
Agent avoids decorative trust theater not tied to real system behavior.

evals.prompt: /design polish the command palette opening animation used hundreds of times per day
evals.expected_output: A motion audit that questions whether animation should exist and requires reduced-motion behavior.
assertions: Agent considers frequency of use before adding motion.
Agent recommends feedback-only or very restrained motion for high-frequency controls.
Agent checks duration, origin, interruptibility, and reduced-motion behavior.
Agent rejects decorative infinite animation.

evals.prompt: /design refactor src/components/Hero.tsx
evals.expected_output: A refactor workflow that includes local scanning, a file-level plan, and rendered proof before completion.
assertions: Agent treats the path as existing UI refactor work.
Agent inspects existing code and design-system conventions before editing.
Agent may use the read-only scanner as evidence.
Agent requires browser, Storybook, screenshot, or equivalent rendered proof when available.
Agent reports blockers if rendered proof cannot run.

evals.prompt: /design make our pricing page look like Stripe's page
evals.expected_output: A visual inspiration workflow that extracts abstract traits without copying trade dress.
assertions: Agent refuses to copy brand trade dress or distinctive proprietary layout.
Agent extracts abstract traits such as clarity, proof, hierarchy, and motion restraint.
Agent translates those traits into a project-specific design thesis.
Agent avoids copying logos, colors, screenshots, or signature component shapes.

evals.prompt: /design create a Three.js product configurator background
evals.expected_output: A conditional 3D interface workflow that uses current official docs and requires canvas proof.
assertions: Agent routes to 3D Interface mode only because the prompt explicitly asks for Three.js.
Agent checks current official Three.js docs for API-sensitive details.
Agent includes resize, pixel-ratio, camera framing, loading, and reduced-motion considerations.
Agent requires screenshot or nonblank canvas proof across desktop and mobile.

evals.prompt: /design optimize this whole app redesign for many many subagents
evals.expected_output: A high-fanout design workflow with a merge captain, read-only exploration lanes, file locks, sequential shared-file edits, and final rendered proof.
assertions: Agent routes to Parallel Design Team mode.
Agent establishes a merge captain before dispatching work.
Agent splits read-only discovery into many independent surface and concern lanes.
Agent prevents same-file parallel writes through explicit ownership or file locks.
Agent uses merge gates before generated docs, shared tokens, or central components are changed.
Agent requires final rendered proof across desktop, mobile, important states, and any canvas or motion surfaces.

evals.prompt: /design clean up the project docs and maybe make the dashboard nicer
evals.expected_output: A gated response that separates docs-maintenance work from user-facing dashboard interface work before choosing a mode.
assertions: Agent applies the Interface gate before loading broad design references.
Agent routes generic docs maintenance outside /design.
Agent keeps only the dashboard interface portion in /design scope.
Agent states the selected primary mode and any secondary modules.

evals.prompt: /design add a single npm version badge to README.md
evals.expected_output: A Badge Surface workflow that loads badge guidance without loading unrelated 3D, AI UX, or broad inspiration references.
assertions: Agent routes the request to Badge Surface.
Agent uses badge-systems guidance and does not preload unrelated references.
Agent verifies marker placement, live endpoint suitability, link target, and placeholder removal.
Agent treats README prose rewriting as out of scope unless requested.

evals.prompt: /design refactor the landing page and save screenshots if needed, but do not commit proof artifacts
evals.expected_output: A refactor/proof workflow that keeps browser artifacts, screenshots, traces, zips, and caches out of committed skill source.
assertions: Agent names rendered proof needs before completion.
Agent avoids committing screenshots, HAR files, traces, heap snapshots, generated zips, and caches.
Agent cleans validation/package noise before finalizing.
Agent does not mutate MCP registry or browser launch configuration.

evals.prompt: /design audit src/app/settings/page.tsx for clipping, focus, and badge/status truthfulness
evals.expected_output: A findings-first UI audit with severity, file/line evidence, proof status, and badge/status signal checks.
assertions: Agent keeps the audit read-only.
Agent leads with findings before summary.
Agent includes concrete file/line references when source files are available.
Agent reports rendered proof status or the exact proof blocker.
Agent checks badge/status indicators for truthful data and source links.

evals.prompt: /design replace our stale README badges with live build, npm, license, and docs badges
evals.expected_output: A Badge Surface workflow folded into /design that previews changes, preserves BADGES markers, and uses truthful dynamic badge sources.
assertions: Agent does not invoke or hand off to add-badges.
Agent inspects existing README badge style and markers before editing.
Agent prefers dynamic endpoints and meaningful links.
Agent skips private or unverifiable badges instead of fabricating status.
Agent preserves content outside the approved badge block.

evals.prompt: /design use ShieldCN style badges for this public package README
evals.expected_output: A Badge Surface workflow that applies ShieldCN style as a provider option without installing or invoking the external shieldcn-badges skill.
assertions: Agent keeps ShieldCN guidance inside /design.
Agent does not run live installs or hosted badge tooling.
Agent uses .svg badge URLs, accessible alt text, and meaningful links.
Agent checks dark/light readability and style consistency.
Agent removes or resolves placeholders before final output.

evals.prompt: /design audit README badges for david-dm, godoc, broken shields, and stale placeholders
evals.expected_output: A Badge Surface audit folded into /design that detects dead badge services, placeholders, provider URLs, and bounded validation needs.
assertions: Agent does not invoke or hand off to add-badges or shieldcn-badges.
Agent flags david-dm.org and godoc.org as stale badge services.
Agent checks badge URLs, alt text, marker boundaries, and unresolved placeholders.
Agent uses bounded validation and does not probe private or credentialed endpoints without approval.
Agent recommends maintained replacements such as pkg.go.dev or current dependency/security status when evidence supports them.

evals.prompt: /design build a REST API for user authentication
evals.expected_output: A refusal or redirect because backend API work is outside design scope.
assertions: Agent detects backend/API work.
Agent refuses or redirects to an appropriate backend/API skill.
Agent does not attempt to build the API.

evals.prompt: /design write a Mermaid architecture diagram for our event-driven backend
evals.expected_output: A refusal or redirect because architecture diagramming is outside this user-facing interface design skill.
assertions: Agent detects this is architecture documentation rather than user-facing UI.
Agent redirects to a diagram or architecture skill.
Agent does not route to visual UI design.

evals.prompt: /design run a full Core Web Vitals and SEO audit
evals.expected_output: A redirect or scoped response because deep web-quality audits belong to dedicated performance/SEO/accessibility skills.
assertions: Agent recognizes Core Web Vitals and SEO as broader web-quality work.
Agent keeps only UI-facing checks in scope.
Agent redirects deep audit work to performance, accessibility, SEO, or web-quality skills.
